name: Houlihan's Baked Potato Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Prick washed potatoes and bake until a fork pierces the center easily. Remove from the oven and allow to cool completely.
3. Remove skin and cut potatoes into 1/2-inch cubes. Set aside.
4. Melt butter in a large saucepan over low heat. Add onions and sauté for 10 minutes or until translucent, stirring occasionally.
5. Avoid burning the onions. Add flour to the onions and butter, cooking for 4-5 minutes while stirring well to ensure the flour is absorbed.
6. In a separate container, combine water, chicken base, potato flakes, dried basil, Tabasco sauce, salt, and white pepper. Stir or whisk thoroughly to remove lumps.
7. Gradually add this mixture to the onions in the saucepan, stirring constantly until no lumps remain.
8. Increase heat to medium and continue cooking until the soup begins to gently simmer.
9. Add milk and heavy cream, stirring until smooth and lightly thickened. Simmer for 15 minutes without boiling. The soup should just simmer lightly.
10. Add cubed baked potatoes and stir to combine. Remove from heat and serve. Top each serving with grated cheddar cheese, sliced scallions, and bacon pieces.
